data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T163115-c0835258/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T163115-c0835258/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T163115-c0835258/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T163115-c0835258/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T163115-c0835258/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-6781181e">
    <title>routing: send MinPower only for review and fallback escalation</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx currently duplicates Fizeau policy intent with numeric power on initial zero-config implementation dispatch. In the current code path, DDx selects the `default` Fizeau policy for ordinary implementation work and then also sets `MinPower` to that policy floor. That makes the request shape `Policy=default, MinPower=7` instead of letting Fizeau fully interpret the selected policy.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:396-401` selects an implementation profile and raises `initialMinPower` to `selection.MinPower` on the first attempt. `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:426-442` then passes `requestedMinPower` into `singleTierAttempt`, which writes it into `config.CLIOverrides.MinPower` at `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:289-294`. `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:241-258` sends both `Policy` and `MinPower` to Fizeau. The same first-attempt pattern exists for `ddx try` at `cli/cmd/try.go:293-322`. Current command tests assert this duplicated shape in `cli/cmd/work_test.go` and `cli/cmd/try_test.go`, so the tests encode the wrong contract.

DESIRED ROUTING CONTRACT
For initial zero-config implementation work, DDx should select an opaque Fizeau policy by metadata and send only that policy. Example: ordinary work selects `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`. Fizeau owns the model choice and may choose a lower-power model inside that policy if the policy permits it.

For implementation fallback escalation, DDx should keep policy intent and add a numeric floor only after evidence of failure. Example: DDx selects `Policy=default`; Fizeau chooses a power-5 model; the attempt fails; DDx retries with `Policy=default, MinPower=6`. If repeated failures or ladder exhaustion require moving policy bands, DDx may reselect the appropriate policy by metadata, but numeric floors are evidence-driven retry constraints, not initial policy duplication.

For review routing, DDx may use `MinPower` immediately when the reviewer must be stronger than the implementation route or when review pairing evidence requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
Change zero-config implementation routing so first attempts do not copy the selected policy floor into `MinPower`. Preserve explicit operator `--min-power`, bead numeric tier hints, reviewer escalation, and retry escalation. On retry, derive the next floor from the actual route power when available; otherwise use the selected policy floor/ladder only as an escalation basis, not as an initial dispatch floor.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hard-code Fizeau policy names. Do not add DDx-side provider/model selection. Do not change Fizeau policy definitions. Do not remove MinPower support for explicit user flags, review escalation, or implementation retry escalation.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts ordinary zero-config `ddx try` sends `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, and no harness/provider/model pins under the canonical Fizeau fixture.
2. `TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts ordinary zero-config `ddx work` sends `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, and no harness/provider/model pins under the canonical Fizeau fixture.
3. `TestWorkZeroConfigRetryAddsMinPowerFloorWithinSelectedPolicy` asserts a failed first implementation attempt with actual power 5 retries with `Policy=default` and `MinPower=6` rather than sending an initial floor.
4. Existing review escalation tests still prove reviewer dispatch can send both policy and MinPower when review pairing or review retry requires it.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTryZeroConfig.*MinPower|TestWorkZeroConfig.*MinPower|Test.*Review.*MinPower|TestSelectImplementationProfile" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:fizeau, area:work, kind:bug, phase:build, reliability</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="736c1be202976ae4ca959b86d06d525ba5f19ea9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T163115-c0835258"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Consumer of the Fizeau Execution Contract</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `d054b8c51`. All ACs satisfied:

- **AC #1**: `TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, no harness/provider/model pins under the canonical Fizeau fixture (`cli/cmd/try_test.go`).
- **AC #2**: `TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, no pins (`cli/cmd/work_test.go`).
- **AC #3**: `TestWorkZeroConfigRetryAddsMinPowerFloorWithinSelectedPolicy` asserts a failed first attempt with `routing_actual.power=5` retries with `Policy=default`, `MinPower=6` (`cli/cmd/work_test.go`).
- **AC #4**: Existing `TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest` (with the implementer-power+1 reviewer pairing) and friends in `internal/agent/execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go` continue to pass — they weren't touched.
- **AC #5**: `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTryZeroConfig.*MinPower|TestWorkZeroConfig.*MinPower|Test.*Review.*MinPower|TestSelectImplementationProfile" -count=1` is green.
- **AC #6**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The implementation: removed `selection.MinPower` duplication in `cli/cmd/try.go` and `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`, and changed `runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go` so that when `report.ActualPower > 0` the next floor is `actual+1` (evidence-driven within-band escalation); the ladder is consulted only when no actual power was reported.
